title: muhlenbergia ligularis
intro: Muhlenbergia ligularis, also known as Mexican Grass or Muhly Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, characterized by its delicate pink or purple flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Muhlenbergia ligularis include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and promote relaxation. Muhlenbergia ligularis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
